description_long: This bungalow features a prominent red hip roof with multiple intersecting sections, creating a visually dynamic silhouette. The exterior walls are finished in a warm beige tone, complemented by white-framed windows distributed across all visible facades. A covered front porch with supporting columns provides a welcoming entrance, while a bay window projects from one side, adding architectural interest. The structure sits on a rectangular plot with a paved perimeter, suggesting a suburban or residential neighborhood setting. The building appears to be a single-story dwelling, typical of bungalow designs, with a layout optimized for ground-level living. The overall appearance is clean, modern, and functional, with ample natural light from the numerous windows.
description: A single-story beige bungalow with a striking red hip roof, white-framed windows, and a covered front porch, set on a rectangular paved lot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance with a covered porch and supporting columns, as well as the prominent red roof and several front-facing windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the bay window projection and a long stretch of the red roof, with multiple windows along the facade.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a simpler facade with fewer windows and the continuation of the red roof's geometric lines.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing additional windows and the side entrance, with the roof's hip structure clearly visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with grouped side windows and bay window on side facade
